observation.summary: Policy bundle loader rules were described as prose while executable evidence lived across internal/policy and daemon config tests.

hypothesis.summary: A manifest can generate the reader doc and prove fail-closed bundle parsing, daemon env wiring, accepted surfaces, and pure policy package boundaries.

execution.summary: Run policybundleevidence to check generated docs, source checks, absent env/remote-fetch surfaces, and compact evidence output.

evaluation.summary: The verifier rejects generated doc drift, missing source checks, unknown fact references, policy package env ownership, and remote bundle fetch ownership.

retrospective.summary: Policy bundle loader knowledge becomes executable security evidence instead of a manually edited rule list.

evidence.proves: policy bundle loader manifest matches generated docs, local source evidence, and absent boundary rules
public CI uploads policy bundle loader evidence
